title: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb

seo.title: Kuzu Tandir: Authentic Turkish Slow Roasted Lamb Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Kuzu Tandir, a traditional Turkish slow roasted lamb dish. Get our authentic recipe for tender, flavorful lamb perfect for any dinner.
longTailKeywords: best Kuzu Tandir recipe at home
seo.h1: Authentic Kuzu Tandir: Turkish Slow Roasted Lamb Recipe
h2Headings: What is Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb?
Ingredients You Need for Kuzu Tandir
Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Kuzu Tandir

seo.imageAlt: A beautifully presented plate of Kuzu Tandir, authentic Turkish slow roasted lamb, garnished with herbs.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich flavors of Kuzu Tandir, a classic Turkish slow roasted lamb dish that promises to impress.

description: Kuzu Tandir is a timeless Turkish delicacy featuring succulent lamb slow-roasted to melt-in-your-mouth perfection. This traditional dish is celebrated for its tender texture and deeply savory flavors, achieved through a simple yet effective cooking method. It's an ideal centerpiece for family gatherings, promising an unforgettable culinary experience that embodies the heart of Turkish hospitality.

ingredients.item: lamb leg or shoulder, bone-in or boneless

instructions.title: Prepare the Lamb
instructions.text: Pat the lamb leg or shoulder dry with paper towels. Make several deep incisions all over the lamb with a sharp knife. This helps the seasoning penetrate deeply.

instructions.title: Season the Lamb
instructions.text: In a small bowl, mix olive oil, salt, black pepper, ground cumin, and red pepper flakes (if using). Rub this mixture generously all over the lamb, ensuring it gets into the incisions.

instructions.title: Prepare the Roasting Pan
instructions.text: Place the chopped onions and smashed garlic cloves at the bottom of a large roasting pan. You can also add fresh rosemary or thyme sprigs for extra aroma.

instructions.title: Sear the Lamb (Optional but Recommended)
instructions.text: Heat a large, oven-safe pan or cast-iron skillet over high heat. Sear the lamb on all sides until nicely browned. This step adds depth of flavor and a beautiful crust. Transfer the seared lamb to the roasting pan on top of the onions and garlic.

instructions.title: Slow Roast
instructions.text: Pour 1 cup of hot water or lamb broth into the bottom of the roasting pan. Cover the pan tightly with aluminum foil. Roast in a preheated oven at 160°C (325°F) for 3 to 3.5 hours, or until the lamb is fork-tender and easily pulls apart.

instructions.title: Uncover and Brown
instructions.text: Remove the foil during the last 30-45 minutes of cooking. Increase the oven temperature to 180°C (350°F) and continue roasting until the lamb develops a beautiful golden-brown crust. Baste occasionally with the pan juices.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the lamb from the oven and let it rest, covered loosely with foil, for at least 15-20 minutes before carving or shredding. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring maximum tenderness and flavor. Serve hot with its flavorful pan juices.

tips: For the most tender Kuzu Tandir, always roast at a low temperature for an extended period. This breaks down the connective tissues, making the lamb incredibly succulent.
Don't skip the resting step after roasting. Covering the lamb loosely with foil allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a moist and flavorful result.
Kuzu Tandir is traditionally served with ic pilav (rice pilaf with currants and pine nuts) or plain rice, alongside a fresh shepherd's salad (coban salata) for a complete Turkish feast.
article: Kuzu Tandir Slow Roasted Lamb is a beloved Turkish Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Turkey. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
